channel.title: About me on Thomas Killestein
channel.description: Recent content in About me on Thomas Killestein

item.title: Making a website
item.description:
This page is intended as a partial tutorial to make your own website. I’ll outline the steps I took to make this page, and add links to additional information. This is a work in progress and wil evolve as I have time to write it.

Building the site

There are multiple different solutions to build your own webpage. For fast load times, easy content management, and general ease of use I built mine using the Hugo static site generator.

item.title: My research interests
item.description:
Machine learning in astronomy and astrophysics

  • Real-time ML/DL workflows for prioritisation and classification of astronomical objects.
  • Data-mining of transient streams, and value-added contextual information.
  • Bayesian deep learning for uncertainty quantification and explainability.

Transients: and what they tell us about the Universe

  • The environments of supernovae, and what they tell us about massive star evolution
  • Observation and characterisation of transients in the optical.

item.title: Observing time/experience
item.description:
2022B

[PI] LT PATT: RISE and shine: exploring the environments of supernovae with high cadence photometry - 12h B-rank time awarded

[PI] ESO: Probing the circumstellar environments of supernovae with high-cadence photometry - 3n awarded on NTT/ULTRACAM

[PI] LT DDT: Probing rapid variability in the very early light curve of AT2022mm with RISE - 3 hours A-rank time awarded

[PI] SALT DDT: Probing the circumstellar environment of SN2021acya with high-speed SALTICAM photometry - 2 hours awarded

item.title: Papers
item.description: For a full list of refereed publications, please see my ADS library

item.title: Talks, conferences, and more
item.description:
Below is a list of all the conferences, talks, and events I have attended each year. Where available, I’ve linked my contributions/the overall course/session page in the hope it’s useful!

2022

  • LIGO CW Group Meeting (invited) - PEGS IV: Updates to the Sco X-1 ephemeris
  • NAM 2022 - convenor of the Machine Learning in Modern Astronomy session.
  • NAM 2022 ‘The lives and deaths of massive stars’ [contributed talk]
  • EAS 2022 [e-Poster] [flash talk slides]
  • ePESSTO+ Science Meeting [contributed talk]
  • Turing Research Software Engineering with Python course

2021

  • NAM 2021 ‘Transients Diversity’ [contributed talk]
  • NAM 2021 ‘AstroML’ [contributed talk]
  • ICIC Fundamental Data Analysis School
  • SCMA VII [e-Poster]
  • LSST All-Hands Meeting [e-Poster]
  • RAS SDM: Machine Learning Applied to Astronomy 2 [contributed talk]

2020

  • GROWTH Summer School
  • Machine Learning for Physicists online course at FAU Erlangen

2019

  • UKRI STFC Introductory Summer School in Astronomy
Building a public, multicolour, legacy dataset for SN2023ixf from community observations
